hero.title: Dental Emergency?
hero.subtitle: Call us now — we see same-day emergencies

steps.title: Call Us
steps.description: Tell us what happened. We'll get you in today.

steps.title: Immediate Assessment
steps.description: We evaluate your situation and manage your pain first.

steps.title: Same-Day Treatment
steps.description: We treat the emergency and plan any follow-up care needed.

steps: Pick up the tooth by the crown (not the root)
Gently rinse with water — do NOT scrub
Try to place it back in the socket
If you can't, keep it in milk or saliva
Call us immediately — every minute counts
Take over-the-counter pain reliever (not aspirin on the gum)
Apply a cold compress to your cheek
Call us for a same-day appointment
Rinse your mouth with warm water
Use a cold compress for swelling

afterHours.title: After Hours?
afterHours.description: If our office is closed and you're in pain, call our main number and follow the prompts for after-hours guidance. For life-threatening emergencies, call 911 or go to the nearest emergency room.

items: Severe facial trauma from an accident
whenToGoER.title: When to Go to the Emergency Room Instead
whenToGoER.description: Go to the nearest ER if you experience:

items.title: Severe Tooth Pain
items.description: Persistent, intense pain that won't go away with over-the-counter medication

items.title: Knocked-Out Tooth
items.description: A tooth that has been completely knocked out — time is critical

items.title: Broken or Cracked Tooth
items.description: A tooth that is visibly broken, cracked, or chipped from trauma

items.title: Abscess or Swelling
items.description: Facial swelling, gum swelling, or a pimple-like bump on your gums

items.title: Lost Crown or Filling
items.description: A dental crown or filling that has come loose or fallen out

items.title: Uncontrolled Bleeding
items.description: Bleeding from the gums or mouth that won't stop after 15 minutes

whatQualifies.title: What Counts as a Dental Emergency?
